_meta.source: Player's Handbook & Dungeon Master's Guide (2nd Edition)
_meta.description: Combat rules: initiative, attack resolution, damage, special maneuvers, turning undead, morale

combat_sequence.description: Order of events each combat round (1 round = 1 minute)
steps: 1. DM determines if any party member is surprised (d10, surprised on 1-3 typically)
2. Determine if encounter distance and awareness allow for actions before combat
3. All combatants declare intended actions for the round
4. Roll initiative: each side rolls 1d10; lower result acts first
5. High-speed actions (spells with speed 1, etc.) may occur before normal actions
6. Resolve melee attacks in initiative order
7. Resolve missile attacks (note: missiles are launched early in the round but hit at strike time)

modifiers: DEX reaction adjustment (for individual initiative variant)
Weapon speed factor (optional rule)
Spell casting time adds to initiative
Charging: acts at end of round but gains +2 to damage
initiative.ties: Simultaneous action — both sides act at the same time

variants.group_initiative: Each side rolls one d10; entire side acts together
variants.individual_initiative: Each character rolls separately, adding DEX reaction bonus

melee_attack.description: Roll d20, add modifiers, compare result to target's AC
melee_attack.formula: Roll d20 + hit modifiers; if result >= (THAC0 - target AC), hit
melee_attack.simplified: d20 roll needed = THAC0 - target AC
modifiers: STR hit adjustment (melee only)
Weapon proficiency (0 if proficient, penalty if not)
Magic weapon bonus
Specialization bonus (+1 fighter specialization)
DEX has no effect on melee attack rolls
Situational modifiers (charging, flanking, higher ground, etc.)

missile_attack.description: Roll d20 for ranged attacks; DEX missile adjustment applies
modifiers: DEX missile adjustment
Weapon proficiency
Magic weapon bonus
Range penalty: -2 at medium range, -5 at long range; no penalty at short range
Cover: target in cover receives AC bonus
missile_attack.ranges: See weapons table for individual weapon ranges (short/medium/long)

damage.description: Roll weapon damage die; add STR damage bonus for melee
damage.melee_damage_bonus: STR damage adjustment added to melee damage rolls
damage.missile_damage_bonus: STR bonus applies to thrown weapons only (NOT to bows)
damage.critical_hits: Standard rules have no critical hit mechanics; optional rules exist in DMG

armor_class.description: AC measures how difficult a target is to hit. Lower AC = harder to hit.
armor_class.range: 10 (unarmored, worst) to -10 (best); negative AC is excellent
armor_class.dex_defensive_adjustment: DEX defensive modifier adjusts AC (negative modifier = better AC)

hit_points_and_death.death: A character dies when hit points reach 0 or below
hit_points_and_death.negative_hp_optional: Optional: characters between -1 and -9 HP are unconscious and dying (lose 1 HP/round unless stabilized); at -10 dead
hit_points_and_death.stabilization: A character at negative HP stabilizes on a roll of 10% per CON point (once per round)

healing.natural: 1 HP per day of rest; 2 HP per complete day of bed rest

hit_points_and_death.unconscious: 0 HP = unconscious but stable (in base rules without optional negative HP rule)

charging.description: Moving at double speed to attack with momentum
charging.requirements: Must move at least 10 ft toward target in a straight line
charging.benefits: +2 to damage roll on the charge attack
charging.penalties: -1 to AC until next round (attacker is off-balance)
charging.notes: Cannot charge through difficult terrain. Pikes and spears set against a charge deal double damage.

set_spear_vs_charge.description: Setting a spear, pike, or similar weapon against a charge
set_spear_vs_charge.effect: Deals double damage if the charger hits the set weapon holder

fighting_withdrawal.movement: Up to half normal movement rate, backward
fighting_withdrawal.benefit: Opponent does not get a free attack (unlike fleeing)
fighting_withdrawal.notes: Must have room to back into

retreat.description: Turning and running from combat at full speed
retreat.penalty: Opponent gets one free melee attack (at +4 to hit) as the retreating character turns their back
retreat.movement: Full movement rate away from combat

overbearing.description: Attempting to knock an opponent down or grapple them
overbearing.resolution: Opposed STR checks or special overbearing table
overbearing.notes: Multiple attackers can assist in overbearing; damage is minimal but opponent may be pinned

grappling.description: Seizing an opponent with both hands
grappling.resolution: Attack roll to grab; opposed STR check to maintain; victim may be pinned
grappling.effect: Victim is restrained; attacker cannot use weapons while grappling

pummeling.description: Punching or kicking an opponent (unarmed combat)
pummeling.damage: 1d2 + STR bonus for punching; 1d3 + STR bonus for kicking
pummeling.stun: On a natural 20 or 5+ over needed roll, opponent may be stunned (save vs. death or lose next attack)

two_weapon_fighting.description: Attacking with a weapon in each hand
two_weapon_fighting.requirements: Off-hand weapon must be smaller than or equal in size to primary weapon
two_weapon_fighting.rangers: Rangers suffer no off-hand penalty (their class ability)

called_shots.description: Aiming for a specific body part
called_shots.effects: DM determines result based on location hit

disarming.description: Attempting to knock a weapon from an opponent's hand
disarming.resolution: Attack roll at -4; if successful, weapon is knocked away 1d10 feet

firing_into_melee.description: Shooting at a target that is engaged in melee
firing_into_melee.penalty: If the attack roll misses the intended target, roll to see if a random adjacent creature is hit
firing_into_melee.rule: On a miss, assign each adjacent combatant a range of die results; one may be hit

cover_and_concealment.concealment: Lightly obscured targets require ranged attack at -2

turning_undead.description: Clerics (and paladins) may attempt to turn undead, driving them away or destroying them.
turning_undead.procedure: Present holy symbol, roll 2d10, compare result to the turning table
turning_undead.evil_clerics: Evil clerics may command undead instead of turning them
turning_undead.paladins: Paladins turn as a cleric of 2 levels lower

table.description: Results: T=Automatically Turned, D=Automatically Destroyed, number=minimum 2d10 roll needed, —=Cannot turn this type at this level
table.turned_count: Roll 2d6 for the number of HD of undead turned/destroyed by a successful result

morale.description: NPCs, henchmen, and monsters may check morale when facing adverse conditions
morale.base_score: Monsters have a morale rating (typically 8–12 on a 2–20 scale)
morale.check: Roll 2d10; if result exceeds morale score, the creature flees or surrenders
modifiers: +1 for each ally still fighting
-1 for each ally who has fled or fallen
-2 if leader is slain
+2 if defending their lair or home

surprise.description: Surprised creatures lose action(s) at the start of an encounter
surprise.roll: Each side rolls 1d10; surprised on a 1, 2, or 3 (unless modified by abilities or situation)
surprise.segments_lost: The surprised party loses a number of segments equal to the surprise roll result (1–3)
surprise.effects: Cannot act, cannot apply DEX to AC during surprise segments
modifiers: Exceptional DEX may reduce chance of being surprised
Elves, halflings, rangers in natural settings: harder to surprise
Invisible or very quiet attackers: +2 on surprise roll
Special senses (infravision, tremorsense) may negate darkness-based surprise

blinded.effect: -4 to attack rolls; opponents gain +4 to hit; 50% miss chance on attack

stunned.effect: Cannot attack or cast spells; all opponents attack at +4 to hit

prone.effect: Attackers in melee gain +4 to hit; ranged attackers suffer -4; movement to stand up uses 1 segment

hasted.effect: Double movement, one extra attack per round; ages 1 year

slowed.effect: Half movement, one attack per 2 rounds; save vs. spell to resist

invisible.effect: Cannot be seen; attackers suffer -4 to hit; surprised on 1–5 on 1d10 (if moving silently)

damage_types.notes: Some creatures are immune or vulnerable to specific damage types. Lycanthropes require silver or magical weapons. Skeletons take half damage from piercing and slashing.

weapon_vs_armor.description: Optional rule: some weapons are more or less effective against certain armor types
weapon_vs_armor.note: This is an optional rule from the DMG. The core game does not require it. See DMG for the full modifiers table if using this optional rule.

kick.notes: -2 to attack roll; may unbalance attacker

bite.notes: Used by humanoids without weapons; rarely effective

headbutt.notes: Requires close range; may stun target
